sourceText: This quote emphasizes the importance of dignity and self-respect, implying that when one loses what they possess or can no longer give, they lose their true value.
translatedText: Wannan furucin yana nanata mahimmancin mutuntaka da mutunta kai, yana nuni da cewa idan mutum ya rasa abin da ya mallaka ko kuma ya kasa bayarwa, sai ya rasa kimarsa ta gaske.

sourceText: Everything is like flowing water; it disappears if you try to grasp it.
translatedText: Komai kamar ruwa ne; ya bace idan ka yi kokarin kama shi.

sourceText: This insight suggests that in a changing world, everything is transient, and excessive attachment can lead to a sense of loss.
translatedText: Wannan hangen nesa yana nuna cewa a cikin duniyar da ke canzawa, duk abin da ke wucewa, kuma haɗakarwa da yawa na iya haifar da hasara.

sourceText: True power lies not in conquest, but in understanding.
translatedText: Iko na gaskiya baya cikin nasara, amma cikin fahimta.

sourceText: This implies that ruling the world is not achieved through physical force, but through the wisdom and insight gained from understanding others and situations.
translatedText: Wannan yana nuni da cewa mulkin duniya ba a samun karfin jiki ba ne, sai dai ta hanyar hikima da basira da ake samu daga fahimtar wasu da yanayi.

sourceText: The 13th-century Mongol Empire's three successive invasions pushed Đại Việt to the brink of extinction, compounded by internal power struggles and social unrest that divided the nation.
translatedText: Masarautar Mongol ta ƙarni na 13 ta mamaye uku a jere sun tura Đại Việt zuwa gaɓar rugujewa, wanda ya haɗa da gwagwarmayar ikon cikin gida da tashe-tashen hankula da suka raba al'umma.

sourceText: Trần Hưng Đạo repelled the Mongol invaders and overcame internal divisions through exceptional military strategy, unifying leadership, and astute political acumen, thereby saving Đại Việt and achieving national unity.
translatedText: Trần Hưng Đạo ya fatattaki maharan Mongol tare da shawo kan rarrabuwar kawuna ta hanyar dabarun soji na musamman, da hada kan jagoranci, da hazikan siyasa, ta yadda za a ceci Đại Việt da samun hadin kan kasa.

sourceText: More fearsome than the enemy's heads is winning the enemy's heart.
translatedText: Abin tsoro fiye da kawunan abokan gaba shine cin zuciyar abokan gaba.

sourceText: This signifies that winning over the opponent's heart is more crucial than mere physical victory, leading to true peace and harmony.
translatedText: Wannan yana nuna cewa cin nasara a kan zuciyar abokin gaba yana da mahimmanci fiye da nasara ta zahiri kawai, yana haifar da zaman lafiya da jituwa na gaske.

sourceText: When the nation is in peril, loyal subjects do not fear death.
translatedText: Sa’ad da al’ummar ke cikin haɗari, talakawa masu aminci ba sa tsoron mutuwa.

sourceText: This emphasizes that in times of national crisis, true loyal subjects must dedicate themselves to the country without fearing for their own lives.
translatedText: Wannan yana jaddada cewa a lokacin rikicin kasa, dole ne masu aminci na gaskiya su sadaukar da kansu ga kasar ba tare da tsoron rayukansu ba.

sourceText: Military strategy lies in comforting the people and strengthening the nation.
translatedText: Dabarar soja ta ta’allaka ne wajen ta’azantar da jama’a da karfafa al’umma.

sourceText: This indicates that the true purpose of military strategy is not merely to win wars, but to protect the people and ensure the stability of the nation.
translatedText: Hakan na nuni da cewa, ainihin manufar dabarun soji ba wai kawai a ci nasara a yaki ba ne, a'a don kare al'umma da tabbatar da zaman lafiyar al'umma.

sourceText: The sudden death of her husband Odaenathus, the role of regent for her young son, and the ordeal of defending the empire alone amidst the constant ambitions of the Roman Empire and threats of internal rebellion.
translatedText: Mutuwar mijinta Odaenathus ba zato ba tsammani, da matsayin mai mulki ga ɗanta ƙarami, da wahalar kare daular ita kaɗai a cikin burin daular Romawa da kuma barazanar tawaye na cikin gida.
